title: Hedyotis selleana
taxonomy: Hedyotis selleana is classified within the Rubiaceae family, a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder or coffee family. This family consists of approximately 600 genera and over 13,000 species. The genus Hedyotis is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. As a member of the Hedyotis genus, Hedyotis selleana is further classified within the family Rubiaceae. The species is also sometimes placed within the related genus Oldenlandia.
uses: Hedyotis selleana is a plant species known to have potential medicinal uses. Its leaves are traditionally used in various cultures to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal value. Research has shown that extracts from Hedyotis selleana have exhibited antimicrobial and antiviral activities, suggesting its potential in treating infections. Further studies are needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.

intro: Hedyotis selleana, commonly known as Sellean's buttonweed, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its ability to reduce oxidative stress and modulate the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Hedyotis selleana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Hedyotis selleana is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to make teas, tinctures, and infusions for the treatment of various health conditions.
